profile.title: Profile Settings
profile.description: Manage your personal information and workspace settings
profile.name: Username
profile.githubLinkHint: Link your signed-in GitHub account @{{login}} to your profile
profile.githubLoginFirst: Please sign in with GitHub first to link your account
profile.githubAuthTip: After linking, GitHub account info (avatar, username, email) will sync to your profile
profile.addWorkspaceDesc: Choose workspace type and configure settings
profile.lastWorkspaceHint: At least one workspace is required

agent.tip: Create more agents via CLI: npx mozi create agent
agent.createDesc: Configure and create a new agent instance
agent.runDesc: Execute agent tasks and view output
agent.listEmpty: No agents yet — create one with the button above
agent.noWorkspace: Add or select a workspace in your profile first
agent.loadError: Failed to load agent or access denied
agent.promptTemplatesHint: Insert a role preset—you can still edit below

promptTpl.codeBody: You are a senior software engineer and coding expert. Answer with clear, maintainable reasoning: include steps when helpful, concise code examples, and common pitfalls. Discuss trade-offs and safety (validation, secrets, concurrency). State uncertainty explicitly and suggest how to verify.
promptTpl.financeBody: You are a senior finance and investment analyst. Ground analysis in public information and stated assumptions; separate facts from opinions; for forecasts, outline scenarios and risks. Avoid illegal or manipulative guidance and remind users to consult licensed professionals when needed.
promptTpl.lawBody: You are a legal research and compliance assistant. Organize information with clear structure; flag jurisdiction, timing, and factual premises. You are not a licensed attorney and this is not legal advice—users should consult local counsel for important matters.
promptTpl.biologyBody: You are a life sciences and biology expert. Use precise terminology with brief definitions when useful; separate established consensus from hypotheses and debates. For medical decisions, defer to qualified clinicians; note strength of evidence when citing mechanisms or data.
promptTpl.historyBody: You are a historian and humanities scholar. Distinguish primary sources, secondary scholarship, and popular narratives; note era, region, and source limitations. Present chronology and causation carefully; avoid presentist judgment and acknowledge multiple interpretations.

agent.skillsCountAria: Skill list counts: all listed skills, and count saved in agent config on the server
agent.skillsTagEnabledTitle: Skills enabled in the agent’s saved config on the server (config.skills). Save the form to sync your checkboxes with the server.
agent.skillsTagTotalTitle: All discoverable skills currently listed in this form (including Tauri on desktop).
agent.skillsRemoteHint: The API scans the agent host’s ~/.Mozi/skills and ~/.agents/skills for any folder that contains a SKILL.md (including nested paths; node_modules and similar are skipped). Set MOZI_USER_HOME if the service host or container home differs from your dev machine.
agent.addSkillByImportDesc: Select a local folder that contains a SKILL.md at the folder root. It is uploaded to the server and saved as ~/.Mozi/skills/<id> (id is normalized from the folder name) and enabled for this agent.
agent.addSkillImportNeedFolder: Choose a whole folder (with a name), not a single file.
agent.importSkillSuccess: Skill imported and linked to this agent
agent.importSkillError: Import failed. Ensure SKILL.md exists at the root after paths are normalized, or the target id is not already present.
agent.skillsHint: Choose which skills to enable, then use Save. Skills are written to the agent as config.skills via the API (PATCH /agents/:id) together with the rest of the form.
agent.skillsEmpty: No skills found. The server only lists directories that contain a SKILL.md file. If you have skills on this computer but the list is empty, the API is probably using a different home (e.g. Docker) — set MOZI_USER_HOME or mount those paths into the service.
agent.readyHint: Click the run button to start the agent

factory.description: Component Factory — build and manage components
factory.createDesc: Create a new component template from scratch
factory.toolkitLoadError: Failed to load toolkits. Please try again.
factory.toolkitEmpty: No toolkits available for this workspace
factory.uninstallFromWorkspaceHint: Remove this toolkit from the current workspace only; registry entry is kept
factory.deleteFromRegistryAria: Delete this toolkit from the registry
factory.registerToolDesc: Register an external tool in the toolkit registry
factory.mcpServerIdHint: Server ID as registered in MCP server management
factory.mcpServerSelectHint: Only active MCP servers registered for this workspace are listed
factory.mcpServerListEmpty: No MCP servers for this workspace yet. Register one in MCP management first.
factory.mcpNeedsWorkspace: Select or activate a workspace before registering an MCP toolkit
factory.mcpCardDesc: Built-in MCP, external gateways, and connection settings
factory.mcpPageDesc: Built-in MCP, external servers, and gateway parameters
factory.mcpSectionBuiltinDesc: Mozi-hosted Streamable HTTP MCP for external clients and toolchains.
factory.mcpSectionGatewayDesc: Timeouts and paths used when proxying to external MCP servers (from service settings).
factory.mcpSectionExternalDesc: Desktop reads ~/.Mozi/mcp.json automatically; in the browser, “Sync from ~/.Mozi/mcp.json” opens a file picker. You can also paste or add manually. Rows are workspace-scoped. HTTP is proxied; stdio runs a subprocess on the API host (command must exist there, e.g. npx on PATH).
factory.mcpDeleteConfirm: Remove this MCP server? Toolkit references may break.
factory.mcpNoWorkspace: Select or activate a workspace in profile settings first
factory.mcpJsonEditorHint: Prefilled from ~/.Mozi/mcp.json on desktop (default template in the browser). Same shape as Cursor: top-level `mcpServers`. On confirm, the file is written only if the JSON changed, then synced to the active workspace.
factory.mcpInvalidJsonReadFail: Could not read the selected file
factory.mcpInvalidJsonOnDisk: ~/.Mozi/mcp.json is not valid JSON. Fix it or use "Edit mcp.json".
factory.mcpJsonUserPath: Desktop: ~/.Mozi/mcp.json (same `mcpServers` shape as Cursor). Browser: after “Sync from ~/.Mozi/mcp.json”, pick your local JSON file.
factory.mcpFileCreatedAndSynced: Created default ~/.Mozi/mcp.json and synced (add mcpServers there, then sync again)
factory.mcpToolkitHttpOnlyHint: Toolkits only support HTTP-proxyable MCP servers (stdio entries are registered but not listed here)

workflow.empty: No workflows yet. Click the button above to create one.
workflow.tip: Use the visual editor to create workflows, or CLI: npx mozi create workflow
workflow.tplBlankDesc: Start from scratch, freely build your workflow topology
workflow.tplSequentialDesc: Nodes execute in order, each step triggers the next
workflow.tplParallelDesc: Multiple branches run simultaneously, merging when all complete
workflow.tplLoopDesc: Repeat a group of nodes based on a condition until exit criteria is met
workflow.editorHint: Design workflow nodes and connections here
workflow.dragHint: Drag components from the right panel onto the canvas, or double-click to add a node
workflow.readyHint: Click the run button to start the workflow

swarm.statsDesc: View cluster performance data and metrics
swarm.readyHint: Click the run button to start the cluster
swarm.statsEmpty: No data yet. Statistics will appear here after starting the cluster.
